portal_system: Washington PDC website (registration, browse/search, guides) + data.wa.gov Socrata open-data layer (bulk lobbyist datasets); legacy AccessHub now redirects into pdc.wa.gov browse/search

seed_urls.notes: PDC home page; top-level navigation to Registration & Reporting, Political Disclosure Data, Rules & Enforcement, About PDC. PDC has regulated WA political disclosure for 50+ years (since I-276 in 1972).
Lobbyist hub page; entry point to Lobbyist Guide, Lobbyist Employer Guide, Grassroots Lobbying, and Public Agency Lobbying Guide.

Registration & reporting hub covering candidates/committees, F-1 personal financial affairs, incidental committees, lobbying, independent expenditures (C-6), and last-minute contributions. Lobbying section has subsections for Lobbyists and Lobbyist Employers.

Lobbyist registration page. Must register within 30 days of being employed to lobby (or before lobbying, whichever comes first); separate registration per employer; valid up to 2 years; electronic filing required; Code of Conduct training certification required (updated Sep 2024).
Political Disclosure Data search/browse hub. Lobbying tools: Lobbyists (agents), Lobbying firms, Lobbyist employers, Public agency lobbying, Grassroots lobbying. Coverage since 2007.

Lobbyist agents search; filter by lobbyist name, lobbying firm, lobbying clients, and year. Returns phone, email, address, certification date. This is also the destination of the legacy accesshub.pdc.wa.gov 302 redirect.
Lobbyist clients/employers search; columns include email, compensation, expenditures, contributions, total expenses, reports. Employers report annually.
PDC Open Data hub; documents the lobbying datasets published to data.wa.gov. Datasets cover the prior 16 years plus current year and are refreshed at least daily.

Socrata dataset (resource id bp5b-jrti): individual lobbyist agents employed by registered lobbying firms; includes agent name, firm, employers list, year, contact info, training-cert flag, bio, photo URL. Page is JS-rendered (chrome only via WebFetch); Socrata API endpoints (/resource/bp5b-jrti.json|.csv) are the Stage-2 fetch targets.

Socrata dataset (resource id mncn-zj8q): one row per agent-to-employer relationship; companion to Lobbyist Agents. Important caveat from PDC: presence in this table only means the agent's firm lobbied for that employer, not that the specific agent did.
Socrata dataset (resource id c4ag-3cmj): per-lobbyist annual totals aggregated from monthly L2 expenditure reports. Effectively the L2 rollup.
Socrata dataset (resource id biux-xiwe): per-employer annual totals (compensation, expenditures, contributions). Companion to Lobbyist Summary on the employer side.
Socrata dataset (resource id 9nnw-c693): finer-grained breakdown of lobbyist compensation and expenses by funding source / employer. Captures the dollar-flow detail behind the summary tables.
Socrata dataset (resource id nuwx-ay5h): filing history per lobbyist (which monthly L2 reports were submitted, when). Useful for completeness audits and missing-report detection.
Lobbyist Guide; covers registration, reportable activities, guidelines & restrictions, deadlines, training, support contacts.

Lobbyist FAQ. Confirms monthly L2 expenditure reports due by the 15th of the following month; covers exemptions (under 4 days in 3 months, under $100 spending), prohibited contingency compensation, and required online filing.
Title 29B RCW 'Campaign Disclosure and Contribution' — the new home of WA disclosure law as of Jan 1, 2026 (recodified from former Title 42 Ch. 42.17A). Lobbying lives at Chapter 29B.50.

Chapter 29B.50 RCW 'Lobbying Disclosure and Restrictions'; 13 sections covering registration (29B.50.010), code of conduct (.020), exemptions (.040), reporting (.050), employer reports (.080), public-agency lobbying (.090), grassroots lobbying (.100), and penalties (.130). This is the primary lobbying statute citation post-recodification.
WAC Title 390 — PDC administrative rules. Chapter 390-20 is 'Reporting lobbying activities'. PDC plans expedited rulemaking to update RCW citations from 42.17A to 29B throughout 390.

flagged.notes: The former primary lobbying statute (RCW Ch. 42.17A 'Campaign Disclosure and Contribution'). Still resolves and the legislature maintains a citation-mapping tool, but each section now carries an 'Effective until January 1, 2026. Recodified as RCW 29B.xx.xxx' note. Keep as a fallback for older documents and PRI-2010 reproducibility, but use 29B as the canonical 2026+ citation.

AccessHub host still resolves but immediately 302s to https://www.pdc.wa.gov/political-disclosure-reporting-data/browse-search-data/lobbyists/agents. Some old AccessHub deep links (e.g., /node/<id>) also still appear in search results. Treat as legacy; canonical search is now under pdc.wa.gov/political-disclosure-reporting-data/browse-search-data/.

flagged.reason: WebFetch returned 403; canonical About URL is /about-pdc
flagged.notes: Use https://www.pdc.wa.gov/about-pdc instead (verified 200). The /about path appears to be blocked or non-canonical. Preserved here so a future Stage-2 doesn't re-discover it as a mystery 403.

flagged.reason: Verified 200 on retry — promote to seed_urls in next pass
flagged.notes: PDC About page (verified content): five-member commission appointed by governor and confirmed by Senate; statutory authority Title 29B RCW + WAC 390; ~95,621 reports filed and 9.4M+ open-data records in 2023. Listed here rather than in seed_urls because it was found mid-pass after the 403 on /about; flagging keeps the agency_about role attested without retroactively editing the seed list.

observations: Two big changes vs the 2026-04-13 baseline. (1) Statute citation: RCW 42.17A was recodified to Title 29B effective Jan 1, 2026, so lobbying-disclosure statute now lives at Chapter 29B.50; the old 42.17A URLs still resolve but each section is annotated as recodified. Any downstream code keying on '42.17A' needs to learn the 29B mapping. (2) AccessHub deprecation: accesshub.pdc.wa.gov now 302-redirects into pdc.wa.gov/political-disclosure-reporting-data/browse-search-data/, which is the new canonical search surface. The Socrata layer on data.wa.gov is unchanged and remains the high-value bulk source — six lobbyist datasets (Agents, Agent Employers, Lobbyist Summary, Lobbyist Employers Summary, Compensation & Expenses by Source, Reporting History) cover the L1/L2/L3 family with daily refresh and ~16 years of history; Socrata pages render via JS so WebFetch only confirms the page chrome, but the underlying /resource/<id>.json|.csv API endpoints are the right Stage-2 targets. 19 verified seeds vs 13 in the prior pass; no hard blockers.
